paper.title: In utero lipid nanoparticle delivery achieves robust editing in hematopoietic stem cells

formulations.targeting_details: Uses Kim's LNP67 formulation for in utero delivery. Dahlman lab collaboration. No additional targeting ligand.
formulations.formulation_notes: Same LNP67 from Kim 2024 Nature Biotech. Applied to in utero context. Collaboration between Dahlman lab (LNP) and Peranteau lab (fetal surgery). PPZ-A10 (CAS 2941268-67-1, SMILES confirmed via Ni et al. 2022 + US Patent 2025/0127727).

experiments.payload: Gene editing cargo (CRISPR-based, details in preprint)

results.hsc_definition: Fetal HSCs (in liver at this developmental stage)
results.liver_notes: Check preprint for liver data — in utero context means HSCs are IN the liver

experiments.notes: Uses Kim's LNP67 in a new context (in utero). Title says 'robust editing' which suggests significant efficacy. Dahlman + Peranteau collaboration. Tag as exclude_from_training (in utero context). Preprint — not yet peer reviewed.

key_findings_for_project: Kim's LNP67 works for in utero delivery — validates LNP67 versatility across contexts
Dahlman + Peranteau collaboration — links two major labs in the field
Tag as exclude_from_training: in utero context is incomparable to adult IV
PPZ-A10 SMILES now known (CAS 2941268-67-1, confirmed via Ni et al. 2022 + US Patent 2025/0127727)

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Different context (in utero vs adult IV).
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Uses SAME LNP67 formulation from Kim's screen. Validates the formulation in a new therapeutic context.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: Cannot place on adult Pareto — in utero context.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Demonstrates LNP67 can deliver editing cargo, not just reporter — important for Kim dataset interpretation.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: PPZ-A10 SMILES now known — no longer an island. Same formulation as Kim LNP67.

limitations_and_caveats: In utero — incomparable to adult IV delivery
Quantitative data needs extraction from bioRxiv full text
